document.title: Japan Test Report
authorityOrJurisdiction: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications
standards: Ordinance Concerning Technical Regulations Conformity Certification etc. of Specified Radio Equipment in Annex 1
Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications notification in Annex "43" of Article 88, Paragraph 1

sourceSummary.oneLine: Rev. 01 Japan test report JR740701-11AE for Laird Connectivity ST60-2230C Bluetooth LE module family, declaring pass results against Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19 and ARIB STD-T66.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as supporting regulatory test evidence for Japan radio conformity assessment of the listed ST60-2230C Bluetooth LE module variants.
keyPoints: Report No. JR740701-11AE is a supplementary report to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AE.
The report states the tested sample was evaluated and in compliance with the above standards.
Model ST60-2230C was selected as representative for final test and only its data was recorded.
Summary of Test Results lists Antenna Power, Frequency Tolerance, Transmitter Spurious Emission, Occupied Bandwidth, Receiver Emission, and Interference prevention function as Pass.
The EUT is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module operating BT-LE at 2402 to 2480 MHz.

entities.name: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications
entities.type: Regulatory authority referenced in test method
entities.evidence: the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications notification in Annex “43” of Article 88, Paragraph 1 and ARIB STD-T66

concepts.name: Interference Prevention Function
concepts.evidence: The identification code shall be 48 bits long

evidence.claim: The document is Report No. JR740701-11AE, Rev. 01, titled Japan Test Report.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: JR740701-11AE Page No. : 1 of 18 Report Version: Rev. 01 Japan Test Report

evidence.claim: The report was initially issued on Feb. 25, 2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Release Record Report No. Version Description Issued Date JR740701-11AE Rev. 01 Initial issue Feb. 25, 2021

evidence.claim: The equipment is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module.
The main model number is ST60-2230C.
The report covers model variants ST60-2230C, ST60-2230C-P, ST60-2230C-PU, ST60-2230C-SS, ST60-2230C-U, ST60-2230C-UU, and ST60-2230C-Q.
evidence.evidenceText: The following models are provided to this EUT... ST60-2230C ... ST60-2230C-P ... ST60-2230C-PU ... ST60-2230C-SS ... ST60-2230C-U ... ST60-2230C-UU ... ST60-2230C-Q

evidence.claim: ST60-2230C was selected as the representative model for final test.
evidence.evidenceText: The above models, model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

evidence.claim: The applied standard is Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19.
Measurement used the Ordinance Concerning Technical Regulations Conformity Certification method, MIC notification Annex 43 of Article 88 Paragraph 1, and ARIB STD-T66.
evidence.evidenceText: Measurement was conducted by the following test method: the test method of Ordinance Concerning Technical Regulations Conformity Certification etc. of Specified Radio Equipment in Annex 1, the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications notification in Annex “43” of Article 88, Paragraph 1 and ARIB STD-T66.

evidence.claim: International Certification Corp. declared the tested sample evaluated and compliant with the stated standards.
evidence.evidenceText: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

evidence.claim: The report is supplementary to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AE.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is issued as a supplementary report to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AE.

evidence.claim: The supplementary changes include additional model and product names, changed applicant and brand name, interface resistance changes, and latest software version update, with original test results remaining the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The modification is 1. Additional model and product names, changing applicant and brand name. 2. Resistance of CONFIG_HOST0/1/2 are different to switch interface 3. Update to the latest SW version After verification, above changes does not impact to original test results. Thus test results remain the same.

evidence.claim: All summarized test result categories passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of Test Results Ref. Std. Clause Description Result 3.2(2)(3) Antenna Power Pass 3.2(4) Frequency Tolerance Pass 3.2(6) Transmitter Spurious Emission Pass 3.2(7) Occupied Bandwidth Pass 3.3(1) Receiver Emission Pass 3.4.1 Interference prevention function Pass

evidence.claim: The EUT frequency range is 2402 to 2480 MHz with 40 total channels.
evidence.evidenceText: Frequency Range (MHz) 2402 ~ 2480 MHz Total Channel Number 40

evidence.claim: The EUT modulation/technology is GFSK at 1 Mbps.
The rated antenna power for BT-LE 1 Mbps is 8.6 mW, measured conducted power is 8.22 mW, and radiated power is 15.63 mW.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating Mode Rated Power (mW) Measured Conducted Power (mW) Radiated Power (mW) BT-LE(1Mbps) 8.6 8.22 15.63

evidence.claim: The tested mode and frequencies for listed transmitter and receiver tests were BT-LE at 2402, 2440, and 2480 MHz.
evidence.evidenceText: The Worst Test Modes and Channel Details ... BT-LE 2402 / 2440 / 2480

evidence.claim: The frequency tolerance limit is +/- 50 ppm.
The occupied bandwidth summary reported maximum 99% occupied bandwidth of 1.232 MHz and minimum 99% occupied bandwidth of 1.222 MHz.
evidence.evidenceText: Max-OBW = Maximum99% occupied bandwidth; Min-OBW = Minimum99% occupied bandwidth; ... 2.4-2.4835GHz 1.232 1H23F1D 1.222

reviewFlags.reason: The source is a laboratory test report and declaration of compliance; it does not provide a regulatory certificate number, grant number, or authority approval status.
Appendix D transmitter unwanted emission pages appear duplicated in the provided preprocessed text.
